key_insight: Many-body DMC reveals that bilayer InSe stacking energies depend on full registry and electronic response, not just the interfacial Se motif, exposing much larger corrugation than DFT predicts.
engineering_relevance: DFT-based moiré and relaxation models for twisted layered materials may significantly underpredict domain formation and electronic reconstruction unless their local stacking energetics are re-benchmarked.
method: Benchmark local-registry energetics of bilayer InSe with diffusion quantum Monte Carlo (DMC).
Compare DMC against DFT for stackings AB, AAr, and ABr that share the same interfacial Se registry.
Assess whether similar charge-density responses imply similar energies, and test the registry dependence beyond atomic motif alone.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that DFT keeps AB, AAr, and ABr nearly degenerate within 1.5 meV/f.u., while DMC separates them by 8(5) and 41(4) meV/f.u., with up to 60(7) meV/f.u. between the most and least stable registries.
watch_item: Results are specific to bilayer InSe, so transferring them to other 2D materials or moiré models still requires system-specific many-body validation.
paper_title: Many-body benchmarking of DFT local-registry energetics in bilayer InSe
